standard_variable.semantic_intent: source-recorded renal replacement therapy fluid-removal event

database_mapping.source_table: std_crrt_device_parameter_event + icu_chartevents itemid split tables + std_non_crrt_rrt_active
database_mapping.source_grain: one source CRRT parameter row or one dialysis-related chartevents row before approved grouping and parent-context filtering
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained source-faithful RRT fluid-removal event row after source grouping and bridge-role classification

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patient-level identifier from approved parent assets or source rows
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: hospital-admission-level identifier from approved parent assets or source rows
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: ICU-stay-level identifier from approved parent assets or source rows

included_scope: CRRT hourly patient fluid-removal volume from approved std_crrt_device_parameter_event
CRRT ultrafiltrate output retained as context-only specialized evidence
hemodialysis output rows overlapping approved std_non_crrt_rrt_active episodes
peritoneal dialysis in/out rows retained as context-only evidence
mapping_specific_notes: Opening v1 keeps hemodialysis output as default bridge evidence but does not smooth session totals across prior hours.
Opening v1 keeps ultrafiltrate output and peritoneal dialysis volume rows as context-only evidence and does not silently add them to the default balance numerator.
Negative and extreme raw source values are retained through caution fields rather than silently disappearing.
adjacent_status_boundary.boundary_reason: This same-name variable represents source-recorded RRT fluid-removal event evidence, not umbrella active-state truth, exact modality episode truth, urine output, generic total output, or hourly fluid-balance summary truth.

event_stream_build_translation.event_time_translation: retain source charttime as the event time and compute relative minutes from ICU intime
event_stream_build_translation.event_value_translation: standardize source-recorded values to fluid_removal_value_ml when the source unit is mL and retain source numeric raw sums
event_stream_build_translation.unit_translation: retain source_valueuom_set and write standard_unit=mL for retained rows
event_stream_build_translation.context_translation: retain source itemid set, source label set, source RRT family class, exact modality context, bridge_role_class, and default bridge eligibility flags
event_stream_build_translation.parent_link_translation: CRRT rows retain parent std_crrt_family_active context from std_crrt_device_parameter_event; non-CRRT rows are retained only when overlapping approved std_non_crrt_rrt_active episodes
event_stream_build_translation.downstream_aggregation_translation: do not smooth, back-shift, difference counters, or summarize; hourly augmentation remains a separate bridge variable
event_stream_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no source-recorded RRT fluid-removal event under this mapping, not absence of RRT support

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not universal proof of no RRT support or no unrecorded extracorporeal removal
validation_contract.unit_check: standard_unit must be mL for all retained rows

caution_rules.negative_value_caution: retain raw negative-source caution rather than silently dropping rows
caution_rules.context_only_default_numerator_caution: context-only rows must not enter the opening default bridge numerator

notes: The retained source is the reviewed local MIMIC RRT fluid-removal bridge event asset.
Opening v1 is an event stream and not a replacement for measured charted ICU balance.
The downstream hourly bridge remains a separate derived variable.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation until a more fully spec-native runner replaces it.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_rrt_fluid_removal_event outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path rather than side SQL or ad hoc rebuilds.
